name: Structural Gingerbread
recipeInstructions:
1. Preheat oven to 350 degrees.
2. Stir all ingredients together in a large bowl.
3. In a 2-quart saucepan, combine corn syrup, brown sugar, and margarine. Keep the mixture constantly over medium heat until the margarine is melted.
4. Stir the liquid into the flour mixture.
5. Mix well with hands until the dough becomes stiff.
6. Chill the dough until it's easy to handle.
7. Roll the dough out to a 1/8 inch thickness on parchment paper.
8. Use patterns to cut into shapes.
9. Bake for 12-15 minutes or until golden brown. Check for air bubbles during baking and poke them with a sharp knife or toothpick.
10. When done, transfer the parchment onto a large cooling rack. Make sure all pieces lie flat.
11. Cool completely.
Note: Dark corn syrup and dark brown sugar may be used and will result in a darker dough.
